title: quercus sessilis var. fruticosa
intro: Quercus sessilis var. fruticosa, also known as the dwarf scrub oak, is a variety of evergreen shrub that belongs to the Quercus genus. The plant is rich in flavonoids, phenolic acids, and tannins, which have been found to possess antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, contributing to its medicinal value. The health benefits of Quercus sessilis var. fruticosa include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce inflammation, and act as an antiseptic, making it a valuable herb in traditional medicine. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various ailments, such as wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues.
